step_by_step_thinking:
Lesions of oral tuberculosis are predominantly characterized by central caseation, the presence of epithelioid cells, and giant cells containing multinucleated structures. The option that stands out as unusual compared to others described here would be hyaline degeneration since it refers specifically to glassy alteration of tissue without active inflammation or granulomatous change. Therefore,
\boxed{B} Based on the consistent description across several sources indicating the hallmarks of oral tuberculosis involve central caseation and cellular components like epithelioid cells and multinucleated giant cells, while hyaline degeneration is unrelated to active granulomatous inflammation,
